nativeRange.habitat: Generally found over sandy areas of lagoons and seaward reefs at depths of roughly 0–49 m (commonly 0–35 m); juveniles tend to associate with seagrass beds. Often observed forming day-resting schools alongside smallmouth grunts (Haemulon chrysargyreum) — an association commonly considered social protective mimicry.

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding primarily on benthic invertebrates — shrimps, crabs, polychaete and peanut worms, small gastropods and bivalves — and on small fishes in the wild. In captivity, generally advised to offer a varied meaty diet of mysis, krill, chopped shrimp, squid, clam and scallop. Juveniles have an active metabolism and have been reported to need several small daily feedings. Constantly probes the sand and rockwork with chin barbels and has been observed to deplete in-tank microfauna populations quickly.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in the mouth

summary: A Western Atlantic / Caribbean schooling goatfish that constantly probes the sand with its chin barbels. Generally considered suitable only for very large display systems; not reef-safe with small mobile invertebrates and small fish.
careNotes: Mulloidichthys martinicus is a medium-large Western Atlantic goatfish (commonly reported around 28 cm, with maxima of roughly 39–45 cm depending on source). The species is rarely seen in the home-aquarium trade and is most commonly maintained in public aquaria and large fish-only show systems. Hobby sources do not publish a precise minimum tank size; given the adult size, schooling habit, and constantly active substrate-foraging behaviour, the figure used here (1000 L, ~240 cm minimum length) is biased toward the larger end of what is reasonable for a small group. Source temperature recommendations differ slightly: FishBase reports a wild preferred range of 23.4–28 °C (mean 26.6 °C); Reeflex lists 20–28 °C; the range used here (22–28 °C) is an average across these. A deep, fine sand bed is generally advised so the fish can use its chin barbels to forage naturally; expect the substrate to be stirred and the water to cloud after feeding. Has been widely reported as an excellent jumper, so a tightly fitting lid or covered overflow is strongly advised. Generally peaceful toward similarly sized tankmates but is a documented predator on small fishes, ornamental shrimps, peanut worms, small crabs and gastropods, so it is commonly considered reef-safe only with caution and unsuitable for tanks containing prized mobile invertebrates. Tends to form day-resting schools in the wild — often in association with smallmouth grunts (Haemulon chrysargyreum), a relationship generally considered social protective mimicry; aquarists are commonly advised to keep this species in a small group (≥3) rather than singly when tank size allows. Wild-caught specimens have been reported to commonly arrive with intestinal parasites and tend to benefit from quarantine and deworming before introduction to the display. Note on identity: many retailers (including LiveAquaria) market the Indo-Pacific Parupeneus cyclostomus under the same trade name 'Yellow Goatfish'; that is a separate species (see yellow-back-goatfish.json) and its husbandry differs. The designated primary source URL (LiveAquaria miscellaneous-fish collection) did not have a species page for M. martinicus, so FishBase was used as primary and the cross-source set is FishBase + Reeflex + Wikipedia + ReefGuide.
breedingNotes: Generally not bred in captivity. FishBase notes maturity in the wild around 17 cm; no published aquarium spawning protocols were located.

primary.name: FishBase
primary.notes: Promoted to primary because the designated LiveAquaria collection URL did not list this species — LiveAquaria's 'Yellow Goatfish' product is the Indo-Pacific Parupeneus cyclostomus, not M. martinicus. Used for taxonomy (Mullidae, Mulliformes), maximum length (44.8 cm TL, common 28 cm), depth range (0–49 m), preferred temperature (23.4–28 °C, mean 26.6 °C), Western Atlantic distribution, benthic-invertebrate diet, and the smallmouth-grunt mimicry note.

additional.name: LiveAquaria (designated primary; no species page)
additional.notes: Designated primary in the briefing. The collection landing page did not list M. martinicus; LiveAquaria's '/products/yellow-goatfish' page sells Parupeneus cyclostomus (Indo-Pacific Goldsaddle Goatfish) under the same trade name, not the Caribbean species targeted here. Reassigned as informational only; FishBase used as primary instead.

additional.name: Reeflex Aquaristic Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Mullidae), maximum length (~39 cm), temperature (20–28 °C), Western Atlantic range (Bermuda/Florida to Brazil, Gulf of Mexico, Caribbean), benthic-invertebrate diet, and 'large display tank only' suitability rating.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Yellow goatfish)
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Mullidae), max size (~39.4 cm / 15.5 in, typical ~28 cm), Western Atlantic + Cape Verde range, schooling-by-day / solitary-by-night behaviour, cooperative hunting roles, and grunt-mimicry association.

additional.name: ReefGuide.org (Yellow Goatfish)
additional.notes: Cross-checked field size (15–30 cm), depth range (2–60 m), and Caribbean / Bahamas / Florida / Bermuda / Gulf distribution.
